log_entries.thought_preview: The malware is a PE32 executable with a size of 1.04 MB and has been detected in three different sandboxes. The tags suggest it's a spreader with runtime modules and an overlay.
The malware doesn't seem to have any network activity, persistence keys, or unique IPs/domains. However, it drops 11 files and creates 7 processes, with a highlighted API call to SetWindowsHookExA.
The malware creates 7 processes, with notable modules including API-MS-Win-Core-LocalRegistry-L1-1-0.dll, imm32.dll, and shell32.dll. It also makes a highlighted API call to SetWindowsHookExA.
The malware exhibits various capabilities, including data manipulation, process creation, registry interaction, and encryption. It also has anti-debugging and anti-analysis techniques.
The malware does not seem to have any persistence keys in the registry.
The malware drops 11 files, all of which are PE_EXE files, into the user's temp directory.
The CAPA sandbox has 19 MITRE ATT&CK techniques and 48 signature matches, but no PCAP or memory dump.
The malware does not seem to have any network activity, including IP traffic, DNS lookups, or HTTP conversations.

log_entries.gap_summary: 9 report-backed behavior(s) are absent from the TTP mapping

- get number of processors
- enumerate files on Windows
- open clipboard
- capture webcam image
- access PEB ldr_data
